initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['transmute']
Prompt family: transmute
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Reformulate this problem into a structured algorithmic strategy focused on state-space reduction. Your reformulation should guide the next agent to develop a method for managing a set of candidate configurations (placements of the known shapes) and systematically pruning this set using the results of different query types. Specifically, describe how to: 1) Represent the current set of valid geometric configurations, 2) Use aggregate queries to eliminate subsets of configurations that are inconsistent with the noisy results, and 3) Use direct drilling to resolve ambiguity in high-probability regions. The goal is to provide a logical roadmap for a search-and-prune algorithm that minimizes total cost.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:


User prompt:
Reformulate this problem into a structured algorithmic strategy focused on state-space reduction. Your reformulation should guide the next agent to develop a method for managing a set of candidate configurations (placements of the known shapes) and systematically pruning this set using the results of different query types. Specifically, describe how to: 1) Represent the current set of valid geometric configurations, 2) Use aggregate queries to eliminate subsets of configurations that are inconsistent with the noisy results, and 3) Use direct drilling to resolve ambiguity in high-probability regions. The goal is to provide a logical roadmap for a search-and-prune algorithm that minimizes total cost.

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S4-ahc030-polyomino-mining
Title: S4. AHC030 / Polyomino Mining

Problem:
Given known polyomino oil-field shapes but unknown placements, recover all oil-containing cells with as little probing cost as possible using drilling, noisy aggregate queries, and final guesses.

Grading target:
Lower probing cost is better.
Contest score is derived from absolute and relative cost.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
